ground_truth.photographer_description: A photo of the Berlin wall, specifically the art piece “Mein Gott, hilf mir, diese tödliche Liebe zu überleben (My God, help me survive this deadly love)” from Dmitri Wrubel at the East Side Gallery, 2009. This is an iconic art piece so it should definitely be able to get this, even without location metadata. It is taken slightly from the side, so if it struggles (which it shouldn't) that should be why.

details.description: A large, iconic mural painted on a preserved concrete segment of the Berlin Wall shows a close-up of two suited male political figures kissing. The artwork fills several vertical wall panels, with weathered, white-painted margins and a dark, worn top edge. Bold Cyrillic lettering runs across the top, and a German sentence along the bottom reads: "Mein Gott, hilf mir, diese tödliche Liebe zu überleben." The painting style is photorealistic with muted flesh tones and black suits. In the foreground is asphalt pavement and a curb; to the right stands a small, sticker-covered information board or placard. The scene is outdoors under a bright blue sky with light clouds, consistent with the open-air East Side Gallery setting along Mühlenstraße by the River Spree.

details.description: A large photorealistic mural painted on a white barrier of a section of the Berlin Wall. The artwork depicts two men in a close kiss, captured in a dramatic, nearly life-sized embrace. Cyrillic text runs along the top edge of the mural, while a German caption at the bottom reads a translation of the scene. The wall shows weathering and the ground is a paved street or sidewalk with a small poster or flyer at the base. The sky above is clear with a few wispy clouds and a faint contrail. The image shows the mural as a well-known, landmark street art piece from the East Side Gallery, with the surrounding area suggesting an urban riverside environment in Berlin.

details.description: Wide-angle photo of the famous mural "My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love" painted on a preserved concrete slab of the Berlin Wall at the East Side Gallery along Mühlenstraße. The artwork shows two suited men locked in a close kiss against a pale blue background, with bold pink Cyrillic text and the German line "MEIN GOTT, HILF MIR, DIESE TÖDLICHE LIEBE ZU ÜBERLEBEN" along the base. The wall’s top edge is weathered with graffiti patina; a sticker-covered information board stands to the right. Asphalt sidewalk in the foreground and a blue sky with a contrail above.

details.description: A weathered section of the Berlin Wall repurposed as street art, featuring a large mural of two men kissing. The work is Dmitri Vrubel's 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love' (1990), depicting Brezhnev and Chernenko in an iconic kiss. It is part of the East Side Gallery, an outdoor public art space along the Spree in Berlin-Friedrichshain.

entities.name: My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love

details.description: A full shot of the iconic mural 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love' (Mein Gott, hilf mir, diese tödliche Liebe zu überleben) painted on a section of the Berlin Wall. The artwork, created by Dmitri Vrubel, depicts a realistic close-up of Soviet leader Leonid Brezhnev and East German leader Erich Honecker engaged in a socialist fraternal kiss. The image is framed by bold text in Russian (Cyrillic) at the top and bottom, with the German translation painted in black capital letters along the lower edge. The artist's name, 'DMITRY VRUBEL', is visible on the left side. The wall itself is weathered concrete with a rounded top edge, set against a blue sky with wispy clouds. To the right of the mural, a small metal utility box or stand is completely covered in colorful stickers. The foreground consists of a gray asphalt walkway.

details.description: A vertical photograph of the famous mural 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love' (often called the 'Fraternal Kiss') by Dmitri Vrubel on a section of the Berlin Wall at the East Side Gallery. The mural depicts Soviet leader Leonid Brezhnev and East German leader Erich Honecker in a socialist fraternal kiss. Below the image, text in Russian and German reads 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love'. The mural is painted on a weathered concrete wall with a rounded top edge. To the left, the artist's name 'DMITRY VRUBEL' is visible. The ground in the foreground is asphalt. The sky above is blue with light, wispy clouds.

details.description: A large, photorealistic mural painted on a preserved section of the Berlin Wall shows two male political leaders in a close kiss, with bold bilingual captions beneath and above the figures. The main caption reads in Russian and in German: "Mein Gott, hilf mir, diese tödliche Liebe zu überleben" (My God, help me survive this deadly love). The mural occupies multiple concrete wall panels of the East Side Gallery; the top edge of the wall is weathered. The foreground is a paved sidewalk/street; the sky is clear above. The artist’s credit to Dmitri Vrubel is visible on the left margin.

details.description: A large outdoor mural painted on a white wall beside a pavement. The central image is a hyperrealistic diptych-like close-up of two men in suits leaning in for a kiss, their faces and lips pressed together; the left figure has dark hair and a prominent jaw, the right figure has lighter hair and is wearing thick black-rimmed sunglasses. The artwork is divided by vertical wall seams and set against a pale blue background. Across the lower portion are bold red Cyrillic characters, and a white banner beneath features a long German sentence in black letters. The top edge of the wall shows weathered graffiti text. In the foreground is a gray asphalt street with a small piece of litter, and a small poster display stands at the lower right corner of the wall. The sky above is blue with wispy clouds and a contrail.

details.description: An outdoor mural on a white wall along a sidewalk under a blue sky. The mural shows a close-up of two older men in suits leaning in for a kiss, one with dark slicked-back hair and the other with lighter hair and glasses. The background is pale blue. Bold red Cyrillic text runs along the lower portion with a black German caption beneath it. The top edge of the wall has graffiti. A discarded poster lies on the ground near the right side. The scene is urban and appears to be in a European setting.

details.description: A full shot of the famous mural titled 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love' painted on a section of the Berlin Wall at the East Side Gallery. The artwork, created by Dmitri Vrubel, depicts a realistic close-up of Leonid Brezhnev and Erich Honecker engaged in a socialist fraternal kiss. The background is a pale blue. Text runs across the top and bottom of the image in both Russian ('ГОСПОДИ! ПОМОГИ МНЕ ВЫЖИТЬ СРЕДИ ЭТОЙ СМЕРТНОЙ ЛЮБВИ') and German ('MEIN GOTT, HILF MIR, DIESE TÖDLICHE LIEBE ZU ÜBERLEBEN'). The artist's name, Dmitry Vrubel, is painted on the left side. The wall itself is concrete with a weathered, rounded top edge. To the right, a small metal utility box is covered in colorful stickers. The ground in the foreground is paved asphalt, and the sky above is bright blue with light clouds.

details.description: A photograph of the famous mural 'My God, Help Me to Survive This Deadly Love' (also known as the 'Fraternal Kiss') by Dmitri Vrubel. The mural, painted on a section of the Berlin Wall, depicts Soviet leader Leonid Brezhnev and East German leader Erich Honecker in a socialist fraternal kiss. The artwork includes text in Russian at the top and bottom, and German at the bottom, which translates to 'My God, help me to survive this deadly love.' The mural is located at the East Side Gallery in Berlin. The photo is taken from a low angle on the sidewalk, showing the concrete wall against a bright blue sky with light clouds. To the right of the mural, a small information stand covered in stickers is visible.
